{"query":"Ilya Sutskever 2025 Age of Scaling over AI progress scientific ideas compute data comment","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1740,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://canartuc.medium.com/ilya-sutskever-just-told-us-the-scaling-era-is-over-3f7891e8016f","title":"Medium","content":"I just watched Ilya Sutskever explain why everything we thought we knew about AI progress is wrong.
When Ilya says “we’re back in the age of research,” this isn’t academic theory.
This is the co-founder of OpenAI, the architect behind GPT-3, telling us that throwing more compute at pre-training is done.
You can consider subscribing to my newsletter to read them first: free!"},{"url":"https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-cofounder-ilya-sutskever-scaling-ai-age-of-research-dwarkesh-2025-11","title":"OpenAI Cofounder Says Scaling Compute Is Not Enough to Advance AI - Business Insider","content":"OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever believes the tides of the AI industry will have to shift back to the research phase.
On an episode of the "Dwarkesh Podcast" published Tuesday, Sutskever, who is widely seen as a pioneer in modern artificial intelligence, challenged the conventional wisdom that scaling could be the key road map to AI's progress.
"So it's back to the age of research again, just with big computers.""},{"url":"https://brief.bismarckanalysis.com/p/ai-2026-ilya-sutskever-and-the-end","title":"AI 2026: Ilya Sutskever and the End of the Age of Scaling","content":"Ilya Sutskever is one of the world’s leading AI scientists.
As of December 2025, he is the CEO of Safe Superintelligence (SSI), a startup he co-founded upon his departure from OpenAI in May 2024, where he was also a co-founder and worked for over eight years as Chief Scientist.
Under his technical leadership, OpenAI, which began as a small non-profit, became the world’s leading generative AI company with a sizable lead over its competitors in both technology and paying user base, a lead it still retains today."},{"url":"https://langcopilot.com/posts/2025-11-26-ilya-sutskever-the-ai-age-scaling","title":"Ilya Sutskever: The AI 'Age of Scaling' Has Ended — Dawn of the Research Era | LLM Practical Experience Hub","content":"In a thought-provoking interview with Dwarkesh Patel, Sutskever—a key architect behind GPT-3, GPT-4, and the transformer architecture—argues that the era of achieving AI progress simply by adding more data and compute power is reaching its limits.
This article breaks down the key insights from the Ilya Sutskever interview, covering his views on 'jagged' model performance, the future of AI safety, and the mission of his new company, SSI.
In the race for benchmark supremacy, researchers over-optimize models on evaluation datasets.
The 'Age of Scaling' (circa 2020-2025), where progress was a direct function of adding more compute and data, is drawing to a close.
With pre-training data becoming a finite resource and diminishing returns setting in, Sutskever argues we are re-entering an 'Age of Research.' The next breakthroughs toward superintelligence will not come from brute force but from discovering new 'recipes'—more intelligent ways to use our vast computational resources for complex reasoning and reinforcement learning.
Sutskever's new venture, Safe Superintelligence Inc.
Something like this must work,' and therefore you have to keep going," he explains.
It is this blend of scientific rigor and artistic intuition that has defined his career and now shapes the mission of SSI.
Co-founder of Safe Superintelligence Inc. with a $1B+ valuation
Whether this new 'Age of Research' will deliver on its promise of safe superintelligence remains to be seen, but Sutskever has clearly drawn the map for a new path forward."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Ilya Sutskever 2025 Age of Scaling over AI progress scientific ideas compute data comment","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://byteiota.com/ilya-sutskever-says-age-of-ai-scaling-is-over-research-returns","title":"Ilya Sutskever Says Age of AI Scaling Is Over: Research Returns | byteiota","content":"Ilya Sutskever—co-founder of OpenAI, former Chief Scientist, and current CEO of Safe Superintelligence Inc—declared in a November 2025 podcast that AI is shifting from the “age of scaling” back to an “age of research.” His argument: pre-training data is finite, scaling has hit diminishing returns, and models “generalize dramatically worse than people” despite benchmark success. While Sutskever claims scaling is over, the industry just committed $7.8 trillion to AI infrastructure through 2030. Either he’s right and the industry faces a reckoning, or he’s positioning his “research-first” company against his former employer’s scaling bet. If the “age of research” is real, algorithmic innovation beats compute access. Someone’s wrong, and it’s a trillion-dollar question. ## The Industry Just Bet $7.8 Trillion That Sutskever Is Wrong. Sam Altman’s justification: “Scaling frontier AI requires massive, reliable compute.” The company projects $20 billion annual revenue by year-end but posted a $7.8 billion operating loss in the first half of 2025. That’s not the behavior of an industry that believes scaling is over.","score":0.80698997,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://medium.com/@stevecohen_29296/from-the-age-of-scaling-to-the-age-of-research-afbda7fb2b5f","title":"Ilya Sutskever’s New Vision for AI","content":"1.   From the Age of Scaling to the Age of Research. Ilya Sutskever, one of the architects of the modern “scaling hypothesis,” spent more than a decade showing that bigger really is better for deep learning. In a long conversation with Dwarkesh Patel, Sutskever argues that the era defined by simply making models larger is ending, and that we are entering what he calls a new “age of research.” He argues that simply scaling the current stack, even with reinforcement learning and smarter inference, will not get us there on its own. It is no longer about compute or model size; the fundamental constraint is now generalization. *   2012 to 2020: the age of research. *   2020 to 2025: the age of scaling. Sutskever notes that the field has effectively shifted from “scaling pre-training” to “scaling RL,” and that in many setups people now spend more compute on RL than on the original pre-training run.","score":0.6882385,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-cofounder-ilya-sutskever-scaling-ai-age-of-research-dwarkesh-2025-11","title":"OpenAI Cofounder Says Scaling Compute Is Not Enough to Advance AI - Business Insider","content":"# OpenAI cofounder says scaling compute is not enough to advance AI: 'It's back to the age of research again'. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever believes the tides of the AI industry will have to shift back to the research phase. On an episode of the \"Dwarkesh Podcast\" published Tuesday, Sutskever, who is widely seen as a pioneer in modern artificial intelligence, challenged the conventional wisdom that scaling could be the key road map to AI's progress. The wisdom goes that the more compute you have or the more training data you have, the smarter your AI tool will be. Sutskever didn't discount the need for compute, stating that compute is still necessary for research and that it can be one of the \"big differentiators\" in an industry where every major organization is operating on the same paradigm. Look out for an alert in your inbox the next time publishes a story!
